meta.title: Nested Circles and Shaded Areas
meta.source: Problem Statement: An even number of circles are nested, starting with a radius of 1 and increasing by 1 each time, all sharing a common point. The region between every other circle is shaded, starting with the region inside the circle of radius 2 but outside the circle of radius 1. An example showing 8 circles is displayed below. What is the least number of circles needed to make the total shaded area at least 2023π?

problem.text: An even number of circles are nested, starting with a radius of $1$ and increasing by $1$ each time, all sharing a common point. The region between every other circle is shaded, starting with the region inside the circle of radius $2$ but outside the circle of radius $1$. An example showing $8$ circles is displayed below. What is the **least number of circles** needed to make the total shaded area at least $2023\pi$?
problem.highlight: Find the least number of nested circles where the total shaded area between every other circle is at least 2023π.

viz_requirements.description: Visualization of nested circles with shaded annular regions, algebraic expansion of ring areas, series summation visualization, and quadratic inequality solving.

actions.description: Draw a circle with the given radius and color. If shaded is true, fills the ring between this circle and the previous one.
Displays the algebraic expansion of (2k)^2 - (2k-1)^2 step-by-step.
Visualizes the summation ∑(4k - 1)π as it builds up.
Plots y = ax^2 + bx + c and highlights the solution at highlightX.
Emphasizes the final answer (e.g., '64') with a visual effect.

nodes.title: Problem Setup
nodes.objective: Understand the shading pattern and the setup of the circles.
nodes.context_from_previous: Student is introduced to the problem of nested circles with alternating shaded rings.

beat_outline.narration_hint: Introduce the problem with the visual of nested circles and the shading pattern.
Show the first two shaded rings (between 2 & 1, and between 4 & 3) in a distinct color.

nodes.title: Calculating Ring Areas
nodes.objective: Derive the area of one shaded ring.
nodes.context_from_previous: Student has seen the nested circles with the first two shaded rings highlighted.

beat_outline.narration_hint: Focus on one ring, show the area calculation.
Animate the algebraic expansion from (2k)^2 - (2k-1)^2 to (4k - 1)π.

nodes.question_hint: What is the area of the ring between radius 4 and radius 3?
nodes.wrong_path_hint: Remember to subtract the area of the smaller circle from the larger one.

wrong_path_acts.title: Reinforce Ring Area Calculation
wrong_path_acts.objective: Correctly calculate the area of a shaded ring.
wrong_path_acts.context_from_previous: Student incorrectly calculated the area of a shaded ring.

beat_outline.narration_hint: Re-explain the area calculation for a shaded ring.

nodes.title: Summing the Series
nodes.objective: Sum the series of shaded areas.
nodes.context_from_previous: Student has derived the formula for the area of the k-th shaded ring.

beat_outline.narration_hint: Break down the summation into 4∑k - ∑1 and simplify to 2n^2 + n.

nodes.title: Solving the Inequality
nodes.objective: Find the smallest n such that Sn ≥ 2023π.
nodes.context_from_previous: Student has the total shaded area formula Sn = π(2n^2 + n).

beat_outline.narration_hint: Plot the quadratic function y = 2n^2 + n - 2023.
Highlight the root at n ≈ 31.555 and the integer points n = 31 and n = 32.

nodes.title: Conclusion
nodes.context_from_previous: Student has solved the inequality and verified the answer.
